But if the detectives thought that this would be the last they'd hear of the man, they were in for a shocker. On October 12th, 2021, over a year and a half after the incident, Detective Schwikt received a handwritten letter from Douglas, while he awaited trial. In the letter, Douglas claimed that he didn't kill Hans and alleged that his old roommate Aaron is the one who killed Hans. Douglas reports that he heard a sound, entered the house, and saw Hans on the floor in his blood. He then went back outside where he alleged that Aaron threatened him and gave him a jacket with some blood on it. After being supposedly traumatized by the event, Douglas claimed to have agreed to take the blame and made a false confession later. When Detective Schwikt interviewed Aaron, he found him to be completely innocent with no involvement in the crime whatsoever. Not long after his arrest, Douglas Taylor entered into the Idaho Security Medical Program, but on October 13th, 2020, after almost four months in the program, doctors declared him fit to stand trial. Then on December 15th, 2021, Douglas Taylor pled guilty to murder in the first degree and was sentenced to 27 years to life on February 14th, 2022. Douglas is currently incarcerated at the Idaho State Penitentiary.
